commit.message:
fix: prevent EXC_BREAKPOINT crash from stale file watcher references (#661)

Fixes a use-after-free crash (EXC_BREAKPOINT / SIGTRAP) that occurred
when file watcher callbacks fired after the associated WebContents had
been destroyed. Four related bugs are addressed:

1. Add `webContentsId` to `WatchEntry` so watches can be associated with
a specific window/WebContents.

2. Implement `cleanupWatchesForWindow` — previously a no-op stub that
iterated watches without stopping any. Now stops all watches owned by
the closing window.

3. Guard `sender.isDestroyed()` in the debounce callback. If the sender
is gone, the watch is proactively stopped instead of silently swallowing
send errors while the watcher keeps running indefinitely.

4. Wire an `AbortController` to the `READ_TREE` IPC handler so
long-running directory traversals are cancelled when the initiating
WebContents is destroyed, preventing late-resolving
`FileHandle::CloseReq` callbacks from firing into an invalid V8 context.

Also calls `cleanupWatchesForWindow` on the window `close` event and
`stopAllWatches` in the `before-quit` handler to ensure all watchers are
torn down during the app lifetime.

Adds a new test suite for `file-watch-service` and expands
`file-handlers.test.ts` to cover the AbortSignal wiring.

Closes #528

---------

commit.message:
fix: chat thread switching loses generation task status

Three root cause fixes:

1. Cache hit path: add _recoverInFlightTasks call (previously only ran
   on cache miss). Tasks that completed while viewing another thread
   were stuck as 'generating' forever when switching back.

2. updateTaskResult: when _taskToThread has no mapping (e.g. after
   page refresh before _scanOtherThreadsTasks completes), fall back
   to searching ALL thread caches instead of silently dropping the update.

3. Orphan detection: after task recovery on cache hit, detect tasks
   tracked in _threadTaskMap but missing from generated_results
   (caused by SSE abort between dispatch and task_created event)
   and trigger DB sync to restore them.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add AudioManager autoload — procedural fight audio system

Closes #11

- Autoload singleton (scripts/systems/audio_manager.gd) managing all fight SFX
- 3 light hit variants + 3 heavy hit variants, procedurally generated PCM16
- Block SFX: metallic ring + dull thud (distinct from hits per GDD)
- Whiff SFX: breathy noise whoosh
- Ignition SFX: rising flame whoosh + power burst
- Announcer tones: ROUND 1/2/3, FIGHT, K.O., PERFECT, TIME
- Timer tick: 1kHz click under 10 seconds remaining
- Background music: looping 4-bar ember drone (bass + drone + kick + hat at 112 BPM)
- 8-player SFX pool with round-robin allocation (per GDD Appendix B)
- ±5% pitch jitter on every hit to prevent repetition fatigue
- 3 audio buses: SFX (0 dB), Music (-10 dB), Announcer (+3 dB)
- Full EventBus integration: hit_landed, hit_blocked, fighter_ko,
  round_started, round_ended, match_ended, timer_updated, announce,
  ignition_activated
- Public volume API: set/get for SFX, Music, Announcer, Master
- Registered as autoload in project.godot (loads after EventBus/GameState)

- handlePrune: Task.WhenAll.Wait(5000) inside task{} -> Task.WhenAny
  with Task.Delay(5000) so thread is freed during the timeout wait

RaylibMode.fs:

  wrapper; runSseListener already returns Task so assign directly,

commit.message:
fix: check deduplication before geocoding in import handler

Previously, pinExistsByMessageId was called only after processMessageIntoPin
succeeded. This meant that if a pin was already in the DB from a previous
import, but AOAI returned no_location on the current run (transient failure,
rate limit, etc.), the message would appear in the failure report even though
it was correctly pinned on the map.

Moving the duplicate check to the top of the loop:
- Skips AOAI calls for already-mapped messages (saves tokens)
- Prevents false 'no location found' reports for pins that exist

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate segfault-prone unsafe code patterns

NativeTernaryMatrix:
- Add input.Length >= _cols guard to DotProductRow (prevents SIMD overread)
- Add _data null checks to all public data-access methods (use-after-dispose)
- Replace bool _disposed with Interlocked.Exchange on int _disposedFlag
  to prevent Dispose/Finalizer double-free race
- Extract ThrowInputTooShort/ThrowObjectDisposed helpers (NoInlining)

ModelSnapshot:
- LoadLazy: wrap in try/catch to release file handles on parse error
- LoadLazy: validate file size >= HeaderSize before mapping
- LoadMapped: validate file size >= HeaderSize
- LazySnapshot.Dispose: use Interlocked.Exchange for thread safety

EntityPrefixRouter:
- Guard stackalloc byte[128] against UTF-8 multi-byte overflow
  (reject entity slugs > 42 chars in char-span overload)

commit.message:
perf(async): remove blocking calls from mailbox agent hot paths

- thread.Join(2000) -> Task.Run + Async.AwaitTask (avoids holding
  agent thread for up to 2s during hard reset)
- disposeTask.Wait(timeout) -> Task.WhenAny + Async.AwaitTask
  (non-blocking timeout instead of synchronous Wait)
- runBuild() calls -> Task.Run + Async.AwaitTask (build can take up
  to 10 min; wrapping frees the agent thread completely)
- Thread.Sleep(500) in DLL lock retry -> do! Async.Sleep 500
- cancelCurrentEval: Async.RunSynchronously -> Async.StartAsTask
  returns Task<bool> so callers can await or fire-and-forget cleanly

Update ActorResult.CancelEval to unit -> Task<bool> and update
EvalCancellationTests to use .GetAwaiter().GetResult() at call sites.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Eliminate redundant text.to_string() on error path (text is already Utf8Bytes)

Memory:
- Pre-allocate JSON Map with_capacity(4) for CDP commands
- Use &str references instead of cloned Strings throughout agent execution loop
